{{Chess Opening Theory/Position |name=Queen's Gambit Declined |eco=[[Chess/ECOD|D60-69]] |parent=[[../|Orthodox variation]] |responses=
* [[/7. Rc1|7. Rc1 · Main line]] * [[/7. Qc2|7. Qc2 · Rubinstein attack]] * [[/7. cxd5|7. cxd5]] * [[/7. Bd3|7. Bd3 · Botvinnik variation]] }} == 6...Nbd7 · Classical variation == With this move Black enters the Classical variation. This is an older and well-studied variation that has stood the test of time, as it is still played to this day.https://www.chessgames.com/perl/chess.pl?yearcomp=ge&year=2020&playercomp=either&opening=&eco=D61 Due to its age, White has developed many responses to it over time. * '''[[/7. Rc1/]]''' is the main line, developing the rook to the to-be-opened c-file. * '''[[/7. Qc2/]]''' is the Rubinstein attack. White develops the queen to watch over the b1-h7 diagonal. * '''[[/7. cxd5/]]''' releases the tension from the center and transposes into an exchange variation. * '''[[/7. Bd3/]]''' is the Botvinnik variation, in which Black gains a few tempi on White's bishop with 7...cxd5 and, later on, Nb6. === History === The classical variation has been played all the way back in the late 1800's and has been deployed by and against the likes of Capablanca, Lasker, Alekhine, Kasparov and many more. == Theory table == {{ChessTable}} {{Chess/theory table |name1=Main line |line1=7. Rc1 c6 8. Bd3 dxc4 9. Bxc4 Nd5 10. Bxe7 Qxe7 11. O-O Nxc3 12. Rxc3 e5 13. dxe5 |name2=Henneberger variation |line2=7. ... a6 8. c5 c6 9. Bd3 e5 10. dxe5 Ne8 |name3=Rubinstein variation |line3=7. Qc2 c5 8. cxd5 Nxd5 9. Bxe7 Qxe7 10. Nxd5 exd5 |line4=7. ... ... 8. Rd1 Qa5 9. cxd5 Nxd5 10. Bxe7 Nxe7 |name5=Botvinnik variation |line5=7. Bd3 dxc4 8. Bxc4 c5 9. O-O a6 10. a4 cxd4 11. exd4 Nb6 12. Bb3 |line6=7. cxd5 exd5 8. Bd3 |line7=7. ... Nxd5 8. Bxe7 Qxe7 9. Nxd5 exd5 10. Bd3 }} {{ChessMid}} {{ChessStub}} == References == {{cite web |url=https://www.chessgames.com/perl/chessgame?gid=1001870 |title=Skipworth vs Bird |website=www.chessgames.com |accessdate=31 December 2025}} {{cite web |url=https://www.chessgames.com/perl/chessgame?gid=1261680 |title=Bernstein vs Capablanca |website=www.chessgames.com |accessdate=31 December 2025}} {{cite web |url=https://www.chessgames.com/perl/chessgame?gid=1007985 |title=Alekhine vs Lasker |website=www.chessgames.com |accessdate=31 December 2025}} {{cite web |url=https://www.chessgames.com/perl/chessgame?gid=1070188 |title=Kasparov vs Portisch |website=www.chessgames.com |accessdate=31 December 2025}} === See also === {{Chess Opening Theory/Footer}}
